title: Alzheimer’s Disease MRI Research
summary: An exploratory MRI classification workflow for staging Alzheimer’s disease progression through CNN and ensemble learning experiments built in Google Colab.
role: Researcher building the preprocessing, augmentation, CNN training, and ensemble-evaluation workflow, then interpreting the notebook as a research record rather than a productized library.

links.label: Open in Colab
links.description: Run the notebook in Google Colab.

pageSections.title: What the repository actually is
pageSections.markdown:
This project is best read as a notebook-first experimentation workflow. The repository centers on a Google Colab notebook that loads MRI image data, builds a TensorFlow pipeline, trains a CNN classifier, and then compares baseline results against simple ensemble variants.

That framing matters. The value of the work is not that it looks like a polished package; it is that the notebook preserves the actual exploratory sequence used to test the problem.

items.label: Input sizing
items.context: The baseline CNN resizes images to 256 by 256 pixels.

items.label: Dataset split
items.context: Training, validation, and test partitions described in the README.

items.label: Comparison layer
items.context: The notebook compares single-model output against simple averaging and draft weighted ensembles.

pageSections.title: Notebook-first machine learning changes what the artifact means
pageSections.markdown:
The README is explicit that the work is exploratory and notebook-first. That makes the repository useful as a case study in how research code actually develops: dataset paths are hardcoded to Drive, model checkpoints are loaded from saved experiments, and the notebook mixes explanation, preprocessing, training, visualization, and ensemble testing in one place.

For this portfolio, that is not a weakness to hide. It is the right way to present the project because it shows the real state of the work.

pageSections.quote: The artifact here is not just a classifier. It is a visible experiment log for how the classifier was framed, trained, and compared.

links.label: Notebook archive record
links.description: Companion archive item for the notebook and run assumptions.
